steps.command: if ping -c 3 -W 2 192.168.1.2; then echo "LAN_REACHABLE:true"; else echo "LAN_REACHABLE:false"; exit 1; fi
steps.plaintext_command: if ping -c 3 -W 2 192.168.1.2; then echo "LAN_REACHABLE:true"; else echo "LAN_REACHABLE:false"; exit 1; fi
steps.description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.
steps.name: LAN Connectivity Check (Ping)

output.stdout: PING 192.168.1.2 (192.168.1.2) 56(84) bytes of data.64 bytes from 192.168.1.2: icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=8.82 ms64 bytes from 192.168.1.2: icmp_seq=2 ttl=64 time=3.39 ms64 bytes from 192.168.1.2: icmp_seq=3 ttl=64 time=3.46 ms--- 192.168.1.2 ping statistics ---3 packets transmitted, 3 received, 0% packet loss, time 2003msrtt min/avg/max/mdev = 3.393/5.222/8.816/2.541 msLAN_REACHABLE:true

steps.command: output=$(hping3 --icmp -i u100000 -c 100 7.0.14.254 2>&1); echo "$output"; loss=$(echo "$output" | grep -oP "\d+(?=% packet loss)"); [ -n "$loss" ] && [ "$loss" -lt 95 ] || exit 1
steps.plaintext_command: output=$(hping3 --icmp -i u100000 -c 100 7.0.14.254 2>&1); echo "$output"; loss=$(echo "$output" | grep -oP "\d+(?=% packet loss)"); [ -n "$loss" ] && [ "$loss" -lt 95 ] || exit 1
steps.name: ddos-icmp

attack.technique_name: Network Denial of Service - Direct Network Flood

steps.description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.
steps.name: Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP)

objectives.name: default
objectives.description: This is a default objective that runs forever.
